```{r}
    weights = "proportional",
    adjust = "fdr"
  )
})

lapply(slopes, kable)
```

##### 6. *Cross-sectional Fe Effects*

::: question

Are there microbiome differences between iron status groups at specific timepoints?

:::

Will help to understand:

- Initial differences at baseline
- Final differences at endpoint
- Whether iron status effects are consistent across time

::: h0

$$
H_{0g}: \mu_{Fe\_high\_base} - \mu_{Fe\_low\_base} = 0
$$
$$
H_{0h}: \mu_{Fe\_high\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_low\_end} = 0
$$
:::

Same as Q2

##### 7. *Time by Fe Interaction*

::: question

Does the effect of time depend on iron status?

:::

Will help to understand:

- Whether temporal changes differ by iron status
- If iron status modifies the trajectory of microbiome changes
- Whether intervention effects vary by iron status

::: h0

$$
H_{0i}: (\mu_{Fe\_high\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_high\_base}) - (\mu_{Fe\_low\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_low\_base}) = 0
$$

:::

##### 8. *Three-way Interaction (Time × Fe × Treatment)*

::: question

Does the interaction between iron status and time differ between treatment groups?

:::

Will help to understand:

- Whether treatment modifies the Fe-time relationship
- If iron status affects treatment response

::: h0

$$
\begin{align}
H_{0j}: & [(\mu_{Fe\_high\_Control\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_high\_Control\_base}) - (\mu_{Fe\_low\_Control\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_low\_Control\_base})] - \\
& [(\mu_{Fe\_high\_FeZnPM\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_high\_FeZnPM\_base}) - (\mu_{Fe\_low\_FeZnPM\_end} - \mu_{Fe\_low\_FeZnPM\_base})] = 0
\end{align}
$$

:::



##### 9. *Sex differences within Intervention arm*

::: question

Does alpha diversity differ between males and females within each intervention arm?

:::

::: h0

$$ H_{0a}: \mu_{female\_CPM} - \mu_{male\_CPM} = 0 $$
$$ H_{0b}: \mu_{female_FeZnPM} - \mu_{male_FeZnPM} = 0 $$

:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
#| results: asis
sex_arm_emm <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ sex | arm_group + time,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1)),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$time <- c("Baseline", "Endpoint")
  return(emm)
})

lapply(sex_arm_emm, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| tbl-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
#| results: asis
# Test sex differences within arms
sex_arm_contrasts <- lapply(sex_arm_emm, function(x) {
  contrast(
    x,
    method = "pairwise",
    ref = c("Female"),
    by = c("arm_group", "time"),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
})

# View results
lapply(sex_arm_contrasts, kable)
```

##### 10. *Overall sex differences*

::: question

Is mean alpha diversity different between male and female subjects (irrespective of intervention group or iron status)?

:::

::: h0
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{EF}{2} - \frac{BF}{2} = \frac{EM}{2} - \frac{BM}{2} \small{(x2)}\\
\Rightarrow& EF - BF = EM - BM\\
\Rightarrow& EF - BF - EM + BM = 0
\end{aligned}
$$

:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex-contrasts
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex contrasts
#| results: asis

emms <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emmeans(
    x,
    ~ arm_group * time + fe_conc * hb_conc * sex,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1), fe_conc = c(12, 25, max(fe_conc))),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
})

lapply(emms, kable, format = "markdown")
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-pairwise
#| tbl-cap: Pairwise comparisons
#| results: asis

#pairwise comparision
lapply(
  lapply(emms, function(x) {
    contrast(x, "pairwise")
  }),
  kable,
  format = "markdown"
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: false
#| label: tbl-sex-fe-diff
#| tbl-cap: Sex group differences
#| results: asis

BF <- c(0.5, rep(0, 3))
EF <- c(0, 0.5, rep(0, 2))
BM <- c(0, 0, 0.5, 0)
EM <- c(rep(0, 3), 0.5)

lapply(
  lapply(emms, function(x) {
    contrast(x, method = list(EF - BF - EM + BM))
  }),
  kable,
  format = "markdown"
)

```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: fig-emm-iron
#| fig-cap: emmeans plots- iron level differences

# Create an empty list to store plots
plot_list <- list()

## Loop through each item in the list
for (i in names(emms)) {
  # Create a plot for each item
  plot <- emmip(emms[[i]], fe_conc * hb_conc * sex ~ time, CIs = TRUE) +
    ggtitle(paste(str_extract(i, "^[^_]*"))) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
    )

  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[i]] <- plot
}

## Arrange and display the plots using ggarrange
ggpubr::annotate_figure(
  ggpubr::ggarrange(
    plotlist = plot_list,
    ncol = 2,
    nrow = 2,
    common.legend = TRUE
  ),
  top = "Change in diversity statistics"
)
```

::: question
**Q2: Same as Q1, but taking intervention arm into account**
:::

$$
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{Arm0EF + Arm1EF}{2} - \frac{Arm0BF + Arm1BF}{2} = \frac{Arm0EM + Arm1EM}{2} - \frac{Arm0BM + Arm1BM}{2} \small{(x2)}\\
\Rightarrow& Arm0EF + Arm1EF - Arm0BF - Arm1BF = Arm0EM + Arm1EM - Arm0BM - Arm1BM\\
\Rightarrow& Arm0EF + Arm1EF - Arm0BF - Arm1BF - Arm0EM - Arm1EM + Arm0BM + Arm1BM = 0
\end{aligned}
$$ 

##### 11. *Sex Effect Over Time*

::: question

Are alpha diveristy rates of changes different between males and females i.e does `time` has a different effect depending on `sex`?

:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: fig-emm-trends-sex
#| fig-cap: emmeans trends- sex differences
#| results: asis

sex_trend <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  trend <- emtrends(
    x,
    ~sex,
    var = "time",
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
})

lapply(sex_trend, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-trends-pairwise
#| tbl-cap: Pairwise comparisons
#| results: asis

#pairwise comparision
lapply(
  lapply(sex_trend, function(x) {
    contrast(x, "pairwise")
  }),
  kable
)
```

##### 12. *Effect of Sex and Iron Status on Intervention arm Differences*

::: question

Does sex and iron status impact the difference between intervention arm at baseline or endpoint?
:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: fe-levels

# Define specific values for iron and hemoglobin that represent clinical categories
fe_levels <- list(
  deficient = 12, # Iron deficient
  depleted = 25,
  sufficient = round(max(fe_conc), 0) # Normal iron status (or use max value)
)

hb_levels <- list(
  severe = 7, # Anemic
  moderate = 9,
  mild = 10,
  normal = round(max(hb_conc), 0) # Normal Hb (or use max value)
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| results: asis
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
sex_fe_arm_emm <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ sex | fe_conc + arm_group + time,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1), fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels)),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$time <- c("Baseline", "Endpoint")
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(sex_fe_arm_emm, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex
#| results: asis
lapply(
  sex_fe_arm_cont <- lapply(sex_fe_arm_emm, function(x) {
    pairs(
      x,
      by = c("fe_conc", "time", "sex"),
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional",
      adjust = "fdr"
    )
  }),
  kable
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: export
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
sex_fe_arm_cont_tbl <- data.table::rbindlist(
  lapply(sex_fe_arm_cont, as.data.frame),
  idcol = "Metric",
  fill = TRUE
)

write.csv(
  sex_fe_arm_cont_tbl,
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/bac-base-iron-sex-CPMvsFeZnPM-B-and-E-EMMs.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

###### Time trend

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| results: asis
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
sex_fe_trend <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emtrends(
    x,
    specs = ~ sex + fe_conc,
    at = list(fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels)),
    var = "time",
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(sex_fe_trend, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex
#| results: asis
lapply(
  lapply(sex_fe_trend, function(x) {
    pairs(
      x,
      by = c("fe_conc"),
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional"
    )
  }),
  kable
)
```

##### 13. *Effect of Sex and Iron Status*

::: question

Are there differences between alpha diversity statistics at baseline or endpoint when iron status and sex are taken into account?
:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
#| results: asis
sex_fe_emm <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ sex | fe_conc + time,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1), fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels)),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$time <- c("Baseline", "Endpoint")
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(sex_fe_emm, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex
#| results: asis
lapply(
  lapply(sex_fe_emm, function(x) {
    pairs(
      x,
      by = c("fe_conc", "time"),
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional",
      adjust = "fdr"
    )
  }),
  kable
)
```

##### 14. *Effect of Sex, Iron and Anemia Status within timepoint*

::: question

Are there differences between alpha diversity statistics at baseline or endpoint when iron status, anemia status, and sex are taken into account?
:::

::: h0
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{EF}{2} - \frac{BF}{2} = \frac{EM}{2} - \frac{BM}{2} \small{(x2)}\\
\Rightarrow& EF - BF = EM - BM\\
\Rightarrow& EF - BF - EM + BM = 0
\end{aligned}
$$

:::

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex-contrasts
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex contrasts
#| results: asis

emmFeHbSex <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ time + fe_conc + hb_conc + sex,
    at = list(
      time = c(0, 1),
      fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels),
      hb_conc = unlist(hb_levels)
    ),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$time <- c("Baseline", "Endpoint")
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)
  levels(emm)$hb_conc <- names(hb_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(emmFeHbSex, kable)

emmFeHbSex_tbl <- data.table::rbindlist(
  lapply(emmFeHbSex, as.data.frame),
  idcol = "Metric",
  fill = TRUE
) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(everything()), type.convert, as.is = T) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(c(Metric, time, fe_conc, hb_conc, sex)), capwords) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = str_remove_all(Metric, "_lmm_FeHb"),
  )
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex-contrasts
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex contrasts
#| results: asis
emmFeHbSex_cont <- lapply(emmFeHbSex, function(x) {
  contrast(
    x,
    "pairwise",
    ref = c("Female"),
    by = c("time", "hb_conc"),
    reverse = TRUE,
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional",
    adjust = "fdr"
  )
})

emmFeHbSex_cont <- data.table::rbindlist(
  lapply(emmFeHbSex_cont, as.data.frame),
  idcol = "Metric",
  fill = TRUE
) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(everything()), type.convert, as.is = T) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = str_remove_all(Metric, "_lmm_FeHb"),
    contrast = capwords(contrast)
  )

write.csv(
  emmFeHbSex_cont,
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/bac-base-end-iron-anemia-sex-subgroup-EMMs.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: fig-emm-iron
#| fig-cap: emmeans plots- iron level differences

# Create an empty list to store plots
plot_list <- list()

## Loop through each item in the list
for (i in names(emmFeHbSex)) {
  # Create a plot for each item
  emmip <- emmip(
    emmFeHbSex[[i]],
    sex + hb_conc + fe_conc ~ time,
    CIs = TRUE,
    plotit = FALSE
  )
  plot <- ggplot(emmip, aes(x = xvar, y = yvar, color = sex, group = tvar)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_line() +
    geom_linerange(aes(ymin = LCL, ymax = UCL)) +
    xlab(NULL) +
    ylab(capwords(paste(str_extract(i, "^[^_]*"), "Diversity Index"))) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    facet_grid(fe_conc ~ hb_conc)

  # Add the plot to the list
  plot_list[[i]] <- plot
}

# Arrange and display the plots using ggarrange
ggpubr::annotate_figure(
  ggpubr::ggarrange(
    plotlist = plot_list,
    ncol = 2,
    nrow = 2,
    common.legend = T,
    labels = "AUTO"
  )
)
```

##### 15. *Effect of Sex, Iron and Anemia Status over time*

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex-contrasts
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex contrasts
#| results: asis
lapply(
  emmFeHbSex_trend <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
    emm <- emtrends(
      x,
      ~ fe_conc + hb_conc + sex,
      at = list(
        fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels),
        hb_conc = unlist(hb_levels)
      ),
      var = "time",
      reverse = TRUE,
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional"
    )

    levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)
    levels(emm)$hb_conc <- names(hb_levels)

    return(emm)
  }),
  kable
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: tbl-emmFeHbSex-slopes
#| tbl-cap: NA

slopes <- lapply(emmFeHbSex_trend, function(x) {
  contrast(
    x,
    method = "pairwise",
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
})

lapply(slopes, kable)
```

##### 16. *Differences between baseline and endpoint with reference to Sex, Iron and Anemia status*

Relevant contrasts (n = 84):

###### A) Sex Differences Within Same Timepoint/Iron/Anemia (24 contrasts)

Baseline Deficient Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Female
Baseline Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female
Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Female
Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Female

Baseline Depleted Severe Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Female
Baseline Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female
Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Female
Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Normal Female

Baseline Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female
Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female
Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female
Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Normal Female

Endpoint Deficient Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female
Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female

Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female
Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Normal Female

Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female

###### B) Iron Status Comparisons Within Same Timepoint/Anemia/Sex (48 contrasts)

Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Severe Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female

Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male
Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male
Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male

Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female

Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male

Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female
Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female

Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Male
Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male

Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Normal Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female

Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Male
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Normal Male
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Male

###### C) Anemia Severity Comparisons Within Same Timepoint/Iron/Sex (72 Contrasts)

####### Normal vs Severe (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male
Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male
Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female
Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male
Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female
Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male
Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female
Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male

####### Normal vs Moderate (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male
Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male
Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female
Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male
Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female
Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male
Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female
Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male

####### Normal vs Mild (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female
Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male
Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Male
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male
Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female
Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male
Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Mild Female
Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Male
Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female
Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Male

####### Moderate vs Severe (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male
Baseline Deficient Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female
Baseline Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male
Baseline Depleted Moderate Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female
Baseline Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male
Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female
Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male

####### Mild vs Severe (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male
Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male
Baseline Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female
Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male
Baseline Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female
Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male
Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female
Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male

####### Mild vs Moderate (12 contrasts)

Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male
Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female
Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male
Baseline Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female
Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male
Baseline Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female
Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male
Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female
Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male

###### D) Temporal Changes Within Groups (n = 24)

Endpoint Deficient Severe Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female
Endpoint Deficient Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male
Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male
Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female
Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male
Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Normal Female
Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Male

Endpoint Depleted Severe Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female
Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female
Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male
Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Mild Female
Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Male
Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Normal Female
Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Normal Male

Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female
Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female
Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female
Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Male
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Normal Female
Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Normal Male

###### Contrast testing

####### Contast filters

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: sex-within-same
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
A <- c(
  "Baseline Deficient Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Severe Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Normal Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Normal Female",

  "Endpoint Deficient Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female"
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: B
B <- c(
  "Baseline Sufficient Severe Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Severe Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Severe Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Mild Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Normal Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Normal Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Normal Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Normal Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Male",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Severe Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Normal Female",

  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Normal Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Normal Male"
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: C
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA

C <- c(
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Endpoint Deficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Endpoint Deficient Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Endpoint Depleted Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Endpoint Depleted Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Mild Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Moderate Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Baseline Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male"
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: temporal
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
D <- c(
  "Endpoint Deficient Severe Female - Baseline Deficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Severe Male - Baseline Deficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Moderate Female - Baseline Deficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Moderate Male - Baseline Deficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Female - Baseline Deficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Mild Male - Baseline Deficient Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Female - Baseline Deficient Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Deficient Normal Male - Baseline Deficient Normal Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Severe Female - Baseline Depleted Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Severe Male - Baseline Depleted Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Female - Baseline Depleted Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Moderate Male - Baseline Depleted Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Female - Baseline Depleted Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Mild Male - Baseline Depleted Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Female - Baseline Depleted Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Depleted Normal Male - Baseline Depleted Normal Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Female - Baseline Sufficient Severe Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Severe Male - Baseline Sufficient Severe Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Female - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Moderate Male - Baseline Sufficient Moderate Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Female - Baseline Sufficient Mild Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Mild Male - Baseline Sufficient Mild Male",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Female - Baseline Sufficient Normal Female",
  "Endpoint Sufficient Normal Male - Baseline Sufficient Normal Male"
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emmFeHbSex-slopes
#| tbl-cap: NA

emmFeHbSexTime <- lapply(emmFeHbSex, function(x) {
  contrast(
    x,
    method = "revpairwise",
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional",
    adjust = "fdr"
  )
})

emmFeHbSexTime_tbl <- data.table::rbindlist(
  lapply(emmFeHbSexTime, as.data.frame),
  idcol = "Metric",
  fill = TRUE
) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(everything()), type.convert, as.is = T) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = capwords(str_remove_all(Metric, "_lmm_FeHb")),
    contrast = capwords(contrast)
  )

write.csv(
  emmFeHbSexTime_tbl,
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/bac-base-end-iron-anemia-sex-EvsB-EMMs.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-cont
#| tbl-cap: Sex Differences Within Same Timepoint/Iron/Anemia (24 contrasts)

kable(
  sex_contrasts <- emmFeHbSexTime_tbl %>%
    filter(str_detect(
      contrast,
      str_c("\\b(", str_c(A, collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
    ))
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-cont
#| tbl-cap: Iron Status Comparisons Within Same Timepoint/Anemia/Sex (48 contrasts)

kable(
  iron_contrasts <- emmFeHbSexTime_tbl %>%
    filter(str_detect(
      contrast,
      str_c("\\b(", str_c(B, collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
    ))
)

```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-cont
#| tbl-cap: Anemia Severity Comparisons Within Same Timepoint/Iron/Sex (72 Contrasts)

kable(
  anemia_contrasts <- emmFeHbSexTime_tbl %>%
    filter(str_detect(
      contrast,
      str_c("\\b(", str_c(C, collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
    ))
)

```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| #| label: tbl-cont
#| #| tbl-cap: Temporal Changes Within Groups (n = 24)

kable(
  temporal_contrasts <- emmFeHbSexTime_tbl %>%
    filter(str_detect(
      contrast,
      str_c("\\b(", str_c(D, collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
    ))
)

```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: save-csv
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
# Convert to data frames and save

write.csv(
  as.data.frame(sex_contrasts),
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/sex_differences_within_timepoint_iron_anemia_conditions.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  as.data.frame(iron_contrasts),
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/iron_status_contrasts_within_timepoint_anemia_sex.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  as.data.frame(anemia_contrasts),
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/anemia_severity_contrasts_within_timepoint_iron_sex.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  as.data.frame(temporal_contrasts),
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/temporal_changes_within_groups_endpoint_vs_baseline.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

###### Iron-deficient Males

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: iron-def-males
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA

defM <- bac_base_end_meta %>%
  filter(sex_catchar == "Male" & fer < 12 & !is.na(fer) & !is.na(hb)) %>%
  mutate(
    Anemia = case_when(
      hb < 7.0 ~ "Severe",
      hb >= 7.0 & hb <= 9.9 ~ "Moderate",
      hb >= 10.0 & hb <= 10.9 ~ "Mild",
      hb >= 11.0 ~ "Normal",
      TRUE ~ NA
    )
  )

defM$Anemia <- factor(
  defM$Anemia,
  levels = c("Normal", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")
)

(p <- defM %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = timepoint, y = diversity_shannon, color = Anemia)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = displayid), alpha = 0.3) + # Individual trajectories
  stat_summary(aes(group = Anemia), fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB")) +
  labs(
    title = "Shannon Diversity Progression by Anemia Severity",
    subtitle = "Iron-Deficient Males Only - Showing Significant Gradient Effect",
    x = "timepoint",
    y = "Shannon Diversity Index"
  ))

p +
  facet_wrap(~Anemia)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: evenness
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
(p <- defM %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = timepoint, y = evenness_pielou, color = Anemia)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = displayid), alpha = 0.3) + # Individual trajectories
  stat_summary(aes(group = Anemia), fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB")) +
  labs(
    title = "Progression of Pielou's Evenness by Anemia Severity",
    subtitle = "Iron-Deficient Males Only - Showing Significant Gradient Effect",
    x = "timepoint",
    y = "Pielou's Evenness"
  ))

p +
  facet_wrap(~Anemia)
```

  
##### 17. *Differences between iron status groups in the intervention arm*

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| results: asis
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
fe_trt <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ fe_conc + arm_group + time,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1), fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels)),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$time <- c("Baseline", "Endpoint")
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(fe_trt, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex
#| results: asis
lapply(
  fe_trt_cont <- lapply(fe_trt, function(x) {
    pairs(
      x,
      by = c("arm_group", "time"),
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional",
      adjust = "fdr"
    )
  }),
  kable
)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: export
#| fig-cap: NA
#| tbl-cap: NA
fe_trt_tbl <- data.table::rbindlist(
  lapply(fe_trt_cont, as.data.frame),
  idcol = "Metric",
  fill = TRUE
)

write.csv(
  fe_trt_tbl,
  file.path(
    RESULTS,
    "iron-status-subgroup-analysis/bac-base-iron-vs-FeZnPM-B-and-E-EMMs.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

###### Time trend

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-sex-effect
#| results: asis
#| tbl-cap: EMMs for sex differences within each arm
fe_trt_trend <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emm <- emtrends(
    x,
    specs = ~ fe_conc + arm_group,
    at = list(fe_conc = unlist(fe_levels)),
    var = "time",
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
  levels(emm)$fe_conc <- names(fe_levels)

  return(emm)
})

lapply(fe_trt_trend, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: tbl-emm-iron-sex
#| tbl-cap: emmeans for iron and sex
#| results: asis
lapply(
  lapply(fe_trt_trend, function(x) {
    pairs(
      x,
      by = c("arm_group"),
      type = "scale",
      weights = "proportional",
      adjust = "fdr"
    )
  }),
  kable
)
```

### Prevalence of Anemia and Poor Iron Status
  
::: question
How does negative iron status and anemia change in the population over time?
:::
  
```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: prevalence of iron deficiency/depletion at endpoint compared to baseline
#| fig-cap: prevalence of iron deficiency/depletion at endpoint compared to baseline
be_meta %>%
  dplyr::select(timepoint, arm, anemia, iron_status) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = !c(timepoint, arm)) %>%
  group_by(timepoint, arm, name, value) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = name, fill = timepoint)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = count), stat = "identity", position = "fill")
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| label: prevalence of anemia at endpoint compared to baseline
#| fig-cap: prevalence of anemia at endpoint compared to baseline
be_meta %>%
  dplyr::select(timepoint, arm, anemia, iron_status) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = !c(timepoint, arm)) %>%
  group_by(timepoint, arm, name, value) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = name, fill = timepoint)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = count), stat = "identity", position = "fill")
```

## Results Summary

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

list_all_results()
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
generate_results_section(directory_naming = "both")
```

## Appendix

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-iron-effect
#| fig-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
# Visualize iron effect
iron_effect_plots <- lapply(names(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc), function(metric) {
  emmip(iron_effect_emm[[metric]], fe_conc ~ time, CIs = TRUE) +
    ggtitle(paste("Iron Effect -", metric)) +
    theme_bw() +
    ylab("Estimated Marginal Mean") +
    xlab("Time")
})

# Arrange plots
gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = iron_effect_plots, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-sex-effect
#| fig-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
# Visualize sex differences by arm
sex_diff_plots <- lapply(names(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc), function(metric) {
  emmip(sex_arm_emm[[metric]], sex ~ arm_group, CIs = TRUE) +
    ggtitle(paste("Sex Differences -", metric)) +
    theme_bw() +
    ylab("Estimated Marginal Mean") +
    xlab("Intervention Arm")
})

# Arrange plots
gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = sex_diff_plots, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-sex-time-effect
#| tbl-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
# Time interaction with sex
sex_time_emm <- lapply(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc, function(x) {
  emmeans(
    x,
    specs = ~ sex | time,
    at = list(time = c(0, 1)),
    type = "scale",
    weights = "proportional"
  )
})
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-iron-effect
#| tbl-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
# Test sex by time interaction
sex_time_contrasts <- lapply(sex_time_emm, function(x) {
  contrast(x, method = "pairwise", by = "time", adjust = "none")
})

# View results
lapply(sex_time_contrasts, kable)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-iron-effect
#| tbl-cap: Contrast results comparing EMMs
# Create interaction plots
sex_time_plots <- lapply(names(alpha_lmms_FeHb_conc), function(metric) {
  emmip(sex_time_emm[[metric]], sex ~ time, CIs = TRUE) +
    ggtitle(paste("Sex by Time Interaction -", metric)) +
    theme_bw() +
    ylab("Estimated Marginal Mean") +
    xlab("Time")
})

# Arrange plots
gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = sex_time_plots, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| label: info

print(sessionInfo(), locale = FALSE)
```
